regions.text: Tallies are simple records that tell how many things have been counted. In a bank, a man who handles money is called a teller.
The table he works at is a counter. He counts money and keeps records in an account book.
Banks make monthly statements to tell people what their account is. Both the person and the bank have the record straight.
To get a statement ready, the banker takes money given out from money put in. tell: give an account. straight: right. FUTURE: will tell. PRESENT: tell(s). PAST: told.
